reason: Both correctly identify alert hygiene as the first fix, but Answer A is substantially more useful without being padded. It gives concrete steps (audit every alert, route non-urgent to business hours, set a paging budget with review, make hygiene part of handoff) and explicitly addresses the rotation-size question, noting six-person weekly rotation is fine once noise drops rather than needing follow-the-sun — which maps well to the key point that the fix is fewer pages, not more rotation. Answer B is a single sentence that states the right first move but stops there, missing the weekly-review-as-bug-tracking practice, the reduced-sprint-commitment point, and any explicit reasoning about rotation structure. Answer A covers more of the key points with real decision-relevant specificity, so it's the stronger answer despite being longer — the extra content is substantive, not filler.
